{"step_by_step_thinking": "Daprodustat is a hypoxia-inducible factor prolyl hydroxylase inhibitor (HIF-PHI). These agents work by stabilizing HIF, which in turn increases endogenous erythropoietin production and decreases hepcidin levels, both of which contribute to improved red blood cell production and treatment of anemia. Document [0] states that daprodustat was approved by the FDA for use in patients on dialysis for longer than 4 months, indicating its use in managing anemia in chronic kidney disease. Document [1] confirms that daprodustat is a novel class of therapeutic agents that raise erythropoietin levels and is currently the only FDA-approved medication in this class for anemia. The approval and mechanism of action directly support its effectiveness in treating anemia, particularly in patients with chronic kidney disease on dialysis. Although it is not approved for non-dialysis patients, its efficacy in the approved population is established. Therefore, based on the evidence, daprodustat is effective for anemia in the specified patient population.", "answer_choice": "A"}
